top_signals.title: Pointed News Quiz | Food & Beverage, Disclosures, Movies
top_signals.summary: David Gura, Christina Ruffini, and Lisa Mateo of “Bloomberg This Weekend” play Pointed! Wager your points, leverage your bets and answer wisely. A new quiz is available to play each week on Bloomberg.com (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Three sons of Iran’s slain leader Khamenei appear at funeral, not his successor
top_signals.summary: There has still been no public sighting or image released of Mojtaba, said ​to have been injured in the ⁠attack that killed his father and the other family members on Feb. 28.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: World Cup fans spent anywhere from $2,500 to $150,000 to see matches—they say it was worth it
top_signals.summary: Soccer fans heading to World Cup matches at MetLife Stadium shared how much they expected to spend with CNBC Make It outside New York's Penn Station.

top_signals.title: Opening remarks by Commissioner Serafin at the Annual EU Budget Conference 2026

European Commission Speech Brussels, 02 Jul 2026 Ladies and gentlemen,
Let me welcome you to this year's Annual Budget Conference.

top_signals.title: Metered electricity demand in the New York ISO falls midday because of small-scale solar
top_signals.summary: An increase in electricity generation from small-scale solar in New York has decreased the midday demand for metered electricity, amid overall declining load in the state. The trend is particularly notable in the early spring (March and April), when solar generation has an outsized impact because demand is relatively low and conditions for solar generation are favorable.
